mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions into equity valuation expansion and lower short-horizon discount rates, which lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate is falling from an already restrictive level.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are not positive on average during months when FEDFUNDS declines, or if the signal's rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation rises above the policy rate, real rates become more negative and investors price a higher probability of continued accommodation, supporting S&P 500 gains over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL growth exceeds FEDFUNDS by at least 1.0pp on a 3-month annualized basis
regime_hint: inflation overshoot with passive policy response
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show positive median 20-day SPX returns, or if the CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS spread has no incremental predictive value beyond FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income expectations and softer earnings demand, which depresses equity prices over the next 20 trading days as labor deterioration feeds into recession pricing.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE jump episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the effect disappears after revision-adjusted evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth combined with a falling unemployment rate marks broad labor-market strength, supporting consumer income and sales growth and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if joint improvement in PAYEMS and UNRATE does not outperform either proxy alone, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not above baseline in the top labor-strength quintile.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO indicates stronger industrial output and operating leverage, which improves near-term earnings expectations and supports S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO monthly change turns positive after two consecutive negative prints
failure_condition: Reject if industrial upturn triggers fail to produce positive median 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal reverses sign during the same revision-adjusted expansion phase.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS captures stronger nominal consumption and revenue growth, which lifts equity earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if rising RSAFS is not associated with higher 20-day SPX forward returns relative to flat or declining sales, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A housing-starts rebound combined with improving retail sales signals broad demand reacceleration, which raises cyclicals and aggregate earnings expectations and pushes S&P 500 higher over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by at least 8% over 2 months and RSAFS is positive month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if the joint HOUST-RSAFS improvement does not outperform single-series effects, or if 60-day SPX returns are not positive in the top decile of the combined signal.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth is positive while INDPRO is flat or improving, the economy is expanding without acute supply stress, which supports margin stability and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 grows year-over-year while INDPRO is nondecreasing over the latest month
failure_condition: Reject if soft-landing episodes defined by this joint condition do not produce positive 60-day SPX returns, or if GDP growth alone explains the effect without INDPRO confirmation.
